name: ARM Pointer Authentication (PAC)
summary: Cryptographic MAC (QARMA-based) over upper pointer bits, signed/verified by dedicated PAC instructions; enforces pointer integrity for CFI / ROP / JOP defence. Universal on Apple silicon since A12 / M1; mandatory in iOS / macOS kernels. PAC sits at the 'pointer integrity, not bounds' point — orthogonal to MTE.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Cryptographic MAC (QARMA-based) over upper pointer bits, signed/verified by dedicated PAC instructions; enforces pointer integrity for CFI / ROP / JOP defence. Five keys (IA / IB / DA / DB / GA) with separate signing domains; Apple extends with up to 9 modifier types and hardware diversifiers in XNU; LR signing on call boundaries for backward-edge CFI.

PAC sits at the "pointer integrity, not bounds" point — orthogonal to MTE's per-allocation tagging and CET's control-flow integrity.

sections.title: Deployment and PACMAN
sections.content:
Universal on Apple silicon since A12 / M1; mandatory in iOS / macOS kernels; available on most ARMv8.3+ SoCs; Linux uses for in-kernel CFI on arm64.

The **PACMAN attack** (2022) showed speculative bypass — mitigated in current Apple silicon. The attack class is conceptually similar to TIKTAG against MTE: speculative side channels can leak the cryptographic tag and let an attacker forge signed pointers without triggering an authentication fault.
